scope: balance/monitor/drift/saturation/alarm cohort regression refresh after the live strict-v3 gate drifted again
learner_facing_quality_improvement: This wave removes a false blame path for learner-facing RAG maintenance: the balance-monitor cohort itself is still individually lintable and absent from the new strict-v3 failure list, so the next queue can target the real schema regressions instead of re-churning safe non-Pilot docs.

verification.global_gate_result: the live corpus no longer passes strict-v3: `[corpus-lint] scanned 2472 files` and reported 115 `corpus_v3_frontmatter` violations. The dominant regression classes are 73 `forbidden_neighbors`/`confusable_with` overlap errors, 39 `linked_paths ... does not match corpus path pattern` errors, and 7 docs missing non-empty `expected_queries`.
verification.cohort_gate_intersection_result: none of the 27 non-Pilot balance-monitor cohort docs appear in the current strict-v3 violation output, so the shared gate failure is external to this lane's active drift/saturation cohort.
verification.retrieval_probe_result: eval and archive artifacts still reference `anti-corruption-mapping-drift`, `semaphore-monitor-basics`, `permission-model-drift-authz-graph-design`, and the revocation-lag bridge family, confirming that learner-facing demand remains real even while the shared schema gate is red.
